danger_signals.visual_form: dense bullet patterns that increase in complexity across loops
danger_signals.meaning: escalating threat level; player must adapt defensive positioning

danger_signals.visual_form: large-scale enemy entities at stage conclusion
danger_signals.meaning: mandatory engagement point; stage completion gate

danger_signals.visual_form: solid stage obstacles in scrolling environment
danger_signals.meaning: instant ship destruction; navigation danger distinct from weapon fire

danger_signals.visual_form: alien-designed structures replacing peaceful planetary landscapes
danger_signals.meaning: occupied territory; world-state under enemy control

danger_signals.visual_form: visual weakening of equipped weapon when hit
danger_signals.meaning: progressive vulnerability; two-hit vulnerability state before destruction

safety_signals.visual_form: defeated enemies and stage progression rewards
safety_signals.meaning: player gains enhanced loadout capability; increased offensive capacity

safety_signals.visual_form: boss defeat triggers advancement to next stage
safety_signals.meaning: safe progression threshold; armory upgrade opportunity
forgiving failure state; tactical repositioning allowed

safety_signals.visual_form: equipped weapons function as damage absorption
safety_signals.meaning: graduated damage system reduces one-hit-kill threat; survivability buffer

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: diverse alien enemy designs across six invaded planetary environments
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: fortress of Armada of Annihilation; occupies final stage; visually dominates Illis system planets
peaceful planetary landscapes (before invasion); player operates as system defender

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: peaceful cloud-covered Cumuluses, functioning space colony Tralieb, populated Urbanite, natural watery Cavern, geothermal Sector 3 Lava Planet
alien aesthetic overlaid on planetary environments; enemy forces visible across all six stages; fortress stronghold dominates final stage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: dense enemy fire patterns; escalating difficulty across stage progression; boss entities guard stage transitions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: recovered D117B weaponry loadout expands after each boss defeat; weapon selection UI reflects accumulated arsenal
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player gains three weapon types per stage; can modify fighter between stages based on level requirements

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: cycle returns to first stage with visual/mechanical persistence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemy patterns intensify; bullet density increases; difficulty escalates without narrative reset

environmental_storytelling.example: six distinct planetary environments (clouds, space colony, urban center, cavern, lava planet, fortress) communicate Illis system geography and invasion scope
Armada-designed structures and aesthetic overlay on peaceful planetary landscapes show territorial conquest and destruction of pre-invasion civilization
top-down stages use enemy and object warping on screen entry to create depth perception; communicates alien dimensional threat and environmental distortion

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: layered background movement in top-down stages
environmental_storytelling.example: communicates scale of environments and player traversal through large spaces; suggests vast invaded territory
final stage stronghold visually dominates as enemy command center; architecture signals centralized invasion leadership and mission objective

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: fires equipped weapon type; can switch between three active weapon categories freely during levels
reduces to weak default weapon version; maintains flight capability; vulnerability increases to two-hit destruction threshold
respawns immediately at death location; respawn mechanic communicates non-permanent failure; allows tactical retry without stage restart
bullet pattern density increases across game loops; communicates escalating threat without narrative reset
mandatory engagement required for stage completion; defeat triggers armory expansion and next stage access

notes: Axelay communicates world-state primarily through environmental design (peaceful vs. occupied planetary aesthetics) and mechanical progression (weapon loadout recovery). The faction dynamic is implicit rather than visually explicit—the Armada of Annihilation manifests through architectural style and enemy design rather than color-coding or sigils. Danger signals emphasize graduated vulnerability (weapon degradation) rather than instant-death mechanics, creating a damage-state communication system. The respawn mechanic and weapon-as-shield system visually communicate a forgiving failure model atypical for shmups. Difficulty escalation through bullet density increases communicates mounting threat without narrative justification, suggesting an endless conflict cycle.
